free_text: Flux measured using black neutron detector
- small (~15 cm height, ~6.4 cm radius) below 1.5 MeV
- large (~40 cm height, ~20 cm radius) above 0.7 MeV
Uranium deposits electroplated onto steel plates.
The data were measured with two 235U foils. The mass
of these foils was updated in 1979 based on a new
isotopic analysis of this material and resulted in a
change of the data by 1.5%. The data were reduced by
a factor of 0.985 as authorized by the experimenter,

Metallic Li evaporated onto Ta (0.025 cm thick)
This data set is treated as a shape data set in GMA
input. The 1977 Nucl.Sci.Eng. article mentions about
an absolute measurement in the same energy range with
a small black neutron detector. But it is not clear
from the GMA input if the absolute measurement is also
taken into account in this GMA data set.

perpendicular to the 0-deg neutron beam direction.
